core_model.structure_summary: A probabilistic convergence statement for multi-indexed random variables: power-law-normalized partial sums over a growing multi-index box converge almost surely, with the rate controlled by tail-integral moment conditions.

variables.meaning: multi-index-indexed random variables being summed (a random field on a multi-dimensional integer lattice)
multi-index upper limit defining the summation box, tending to infinity
normalization exponent r controlling the power-law scaling of the sum
moment/order exponent p entering the convergence-rate conditions

notes: The paper is a pure probability-theory result (Marcinkiewicz-Zygmund strong law of large numbers and convergence rates for sums of a random field, applied to its discrete Fourier transform). The controlled feature vocabulary is dynamics/physics-oriented and fits poorly; only power-law normalization (scale_invariance) and the summation of random terms (additive_noise) genuinely apply, so the tag list is intentionally short. Extracted equations are the moment/tail-integral inequalities used in the proof, not a single defining model equation.
